title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object Properties

statement.markdown: You are given a React memoized component that re-renders unnecessarily when properties of an object change. The component expects an object with a unique id and some additional information. The goal is to modify the implementation so that the component memoizes the object based on its id, preventing re-renders when the object's properties change.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an object with a unique id and some additional information.
statement.outputFormat: The component should return the memoized object based on its id.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the id changes.

examples.explanation: The component should memoize the object based on its id and return the same object when the id is the same.
The component should memoize a new object when the id changes.

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the object based on its id.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The component should memoize the object based on its id to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use a simple cache to store the memoized objects.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the object based on its id.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook with a custom equality function to memoize the object based on its id.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness lies in the fact that the component re-renders only when the id changes.
commonMistakes: Mistaking the object's properties for its id
